Fungal infection.description: A fungal infection caused by various fungi affecting skin, nails, or internal organs.
precautions: Use antifungal powder in moist areas
Fungal infection.treatment: Antifungal creams or oral antifungal medications prescribed by a doctor.
Fungal infection.diet: Reduce sugar intake, eat probiotic-rich foods like yogurt, avoid processed foods.

Allergy.description: An immune system reaction to substances (allergens) that are usually harmless.
Allergy.treatment: Antihistamines, corticosteroids, decongestants, immunotherapy for long-term.
Allergy.diet: Anti-inflammatory foods, avoid known food triggers, eat quercetin-rich foods (apples, onions).

GERD.description: Gastroesophageal reflux disease — chronic acid reflux where stomach acid flows into the esophagus.
GERD.treatment: Antacids, PPIs (omeprazole), H2 blockers, lifestyle changes.
GERD.diet: Avoid coffee, alcohol, citrus, spicy foods. Eat oatmeal, bananas, ginger, green vegetables.

Chronic cholestasis.description: A liver condition where bile flow is reduced or blocked, causing bile to accumulate.
Chronic cholestasis.treatment: Ursodeoxycholic acid, cholestyramine for itch, treat underlying cause.
Chronic cholestasis.diet: Low-fat diet, fat-soluble vitamins A/D/E/K supplementation, avoid alcohol.

Drug Reaction.description: An adverse reaction to medication, ranging from mild skin rash to life-threatening anaphylaxis.
precautions: Always inform doctors of all allergies
Report any new symptoms after starting medication
Drug Reaction.treatment: Stop the offending drug immediately, antihistamines, corticosteroids, epinephrine for anaphylaxis.
Drug Reaction.diet: Stay well-hydrated, avoid alcohol, eat light easily digestible foods during recovery.

Peptic ulcer diseae.description: Sores (ulcers) that develop on the lining of the stomach or upper small intestine.
Peptic ulcer diseae.treatment: PPIs, H2 blockers, antibiotics if H. pylori is detected, antacids.
Peptic ulcer diseae.diet: Avoid spicy, acidic, fried foods. Eat fiber-rich foods, cabbage juice, honey, probiotic yogurt.

AIDS.description: Advanced stage of HIV infection severely compromising the immune system.
AIDS.treatment: Antiretroviral therapy (ART) — lifelong treatment; treat opportunistic infections.
AIDS.diet: High protein, high calorie, nutrient-dense foods; food safety is critical due to low immunity.

Diabetes.description: Chronic metabolic disease where the body cannot properly regulate blood sugar levels.
precautions: Avoid refined sugar and white flour
Diabetes.treatment: Type 1: Insulin therapy. Type 2: Oral medications (metformin), insulin, lifestyle changes.
Diabetes.diet: Low glycemic index foods, high fiber, lean proteins. Avoid sugary drinks, white rice, refined carbs.

Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines usually caused by viral or bacterial infection.
precautions: Wash hands before eating and after toilet
Gastroenteritis.treatment: Oral rehydration therapy (ORS), rest, antibiotics only if bacterial cause confirmed.
Gastroenteritis.diet: BRAT diet: bananas, rice, applesauce, toast. Clear broths, coconut water, avoid dairy and spicy food.

Bronchial Asthma.description: Chronic inflammation of the airways making breathing difficult with recurring attacks.
Bronchial Asthma.treatment: Short-acting bronchodilators for attacks, inhaled corticosteroids for prevention.
Bronchial Asthma.diet: Omega-3 rich foods (fish, flaxseed), vitamin D, magnesium-rich foods; avoid sulfites and processed foods.

Hypertension.description: Persistently high blood pressure that damages arteries and increases risk of heart disease.
Hypertension.treatment: ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, diuretics, calcium channel blockers, DASH diet.
Hypertension.diet: DASH diet: fruits, vegetables, whole grains, low-fat dairy. Limit salt, alcohol, and saturated fat.

Migraine.description: Recurring severe headache attacks often with nausea, vomiting, and sensitivity to light/sound.
precautions: Keep a migraine diary to identify triggers
Reduce screen time and bright light exposure
Migraine.treatment: Triptans (sumatriptan) for acute attacks, preventive medications (topiramate, beta-blockers).
Migraine.diet: Avoid red wine, aged cheese, MSG, caffeine withdrawal. Stay hydrated, regular meal times.

Cervical spondylosis.description: Age-related degeneration of spinal discs and vertebrae in the neck causing pain and stiffness.
precautions: Avoid carrying heavy loads on head/shoulders
Cervical spondylosis.treatment: Physical therapy, NSAIDs, muscle relaxants, cervical collar, surgery in severe cases.
Cervical spondylosis.diet: Calcium and vitamin D-rich foods, omega-3 anti-inflammatory foods, turmeric and ginger.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).description: Loss of muscle function in part of the body caused by bleeding within the brain.
precautions: Seek emergency care immediately for stroke symptoms
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).treatment: EMERGENCY surgery, clot removal, blood pressure management, intensive rehabilitation therapy.
Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).diet: Low-sodium, heart-healthy diet. Adequate protein for muscle recovery, antioxidant-rich foods.

Jaundice.description: Yellowing of skin and eyes caused by excess bilirubin, indicating liver or bile duct problems.
Jaundice.treatment: Treat the underlying cause (hepatitis, gallstones, etc.); supportive hydration and nutrition.
Jaundice.diet: High carbohydrate, very low fat diet. Fresh fruits, vegetables, sugarcane juice, coconut water.

Malaria.description: Life-threatening parasitic disease spread by Anopheles mosquito bites caused by Plasmodium.
precautions: Apply DEET repellent on exposed skin
Take prescribed antimalarials when in endemic areas
Malaria.treatment: Artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs); chloroquine in sensitive regions.
Malaria.diet: High calorie, high protein foods; plenty of fluids and electrolytes; avoid solid foods during high fever.

Chicken pox.description: Highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy blister-like rash all over the body.
precautions: Isolate until all sores crust over
Chicken pox.treatment: Antihistamines for itch, calamine lotion, acyclovir for severe or at-risk cases.
Chicken pox.diet: Soft cool foods (yogurt, smoothies), plenty of fluids. Avoid salty, spicy, acidic foods.

Dengue.description: Mosquito-borne viral disease causing high fever, severe headache, joint pain, and rash.
Dengue.treatment: Supportive care only: rest, hydration, paracetamol for fever. AVOID aspirin and ibuprofen.
Dengue.diet: Papaya leaf extract juice, pomegranate juice, coconut water, ORS, kiwi (for platelet boost).

Typhoid.description: Serious bacterial infection from Salmonella typhi causing prolonged fever and systemic illness.
precautions: Drink only boiled or bottled water
Get typhoid vaccine before travel to endemic areas
Typhoid.treatment: Antibiotics: ciprofloxacin, azithromycin, or ceftriaxone; 7-14 days course; rest and hydration.
Typhoid.diet: High calorie soft foods: boiled rice, bananas, boiled potatoes, soups. Avoid raw/spicy/fried foods.

hepatitis A.description: Viral liver infection spread through contaminated food and water, usually self-limiting.
precautions: Wash hands before eating and after toilet
hepatitis A.treatment: Supportive care, rest, adequate nutrition, avoid alcohol. No specific antiviral needed.
hepatitis A.diet: High carbohydrate, very low fat diet. Fresh juices, fruits, vegetables. Absolutely no alcohol.

Hepatitis B.description: Viral liver infection spread through blood, sexual contact, and mother-to-child transmission.
Hepatitis B.treatment: Antiviral medications (tenofovir, entecavir) for chronic cases; regular liver monitoring.
Hepatitis B.diet: Low fat, high protein, avoid alcohol and raw shellfish. Liver-supportive foods: beets, carrots, leafy greens.

Hepatitis C.description: Viral liver infection primarily spread through blood-to-blood contact, often leading to chronic liver disease.
precautions: Never share needles or drug equipment
Hepatitis C.treatment: Direct-acting antivirals (DAAs) — 8-12 week treatment with >95% cure rate.
Hepatitis C.diet: Liver-friendly diet: avoid alcohol, processed foods, excess sodium. Eat fruits, vegetables, whole grains.

Hepatitis D.description: Liver infection caused by Hepatitis D virus, only occurs in people already infected with Hepatitis B.
precautions: Hepatitis B vaccination also prevents D
Hepatitis D.treatment: Pegylated interferon-alpha; treat underlying Hepatitis B infection.
Hepatitis D.diet: Same as Hepatitis B: low fat, no alcohol, high antioxidant foods.

Hepatitis E.description: Liver infection from Hepatitis E virus, spread through contaminated water, common in developing regions.
precautions: Drink boiled or purified water only
Hepatitis E.treatment: Usually self-limiting in 4-6 weeks; rest and hydration; ribavirin for severe/chronic cases.
Hepatitis E.diet: Low fat, easily digestible foods. Avoid alcohol completely. Stay well hydrated.

Alcoholic hepatitis.description: Liver inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption over time.
precautions: Stop drinking alcohol immediately and permanently
Alcoholic hepatitis.treatment: Complete alcohol abstinence, corticosteroids, nutritional support, liver transplant in end-stage.
Alcoholic hepatitis.diet: High calorie, high protein diet. Vitamins B1 (thiamine), B12, folate supplementation. No alcohol ever.

Tuberculosis.description: Contagious bacterial infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, primarily affecting lungs.
precautions: Complete the FULL 6-month antibiotic course without missing doses
Cover mouth with elbow when coughing
Get tested if exposed to TB patient
Tuberculosis.treatment: DOTS therapy: 6-9 months of HRZE antibiotics (Isoniazid, Rifampicin, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol).
Tuberculosis.diet: High calorie, high protein diet. Vitamin D, zinc, iron, B-vitamin supplementation. Avoid alcohol.

Common Cold.description: Mild viral respiratory infection of the upper respiratory tract, usually caused by rhinovirus.
precautions: Avoid close contact with infected people
Common Cold.treatment: Symptomatic only: rest, hydration, decongestants, throat lozenges, saline nasal spray.
Common Cold.diet: Warm soups and broths, herbal teas with honey, citrus fruits, ginger-garlic, hot water with turmeric.

Pneumonia.description: Serious infection inflaming air sacs in lungs, filling them with fluid or pus.
precautions: Seek early medical care for respiratory symptoms
Pneumonia.treatment: Antibiotics for bacterial, antivirals for viral pneumonia. Hospitalization if oxygen levels drop.
Pneumonia.diet: Warm fluids, broths, vitamin C rich fruits, easily digestible light foods. Avoid dairy if congested.

Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).description: Swollen and inflamed veins in the rectum and anus causing pain, itching, and bleeding.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).treatment: Sitz baths, topical creams (hydrocortisone), fiber supplements, sclerotherapy, surgery if severe.
Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).diet: High fiber: whole grains, legumes, vegetables, fruits. Drink 2-3L water daily. Avoid spicy food.

Heart attack.description: Emergency: blockage of blood supply to heart muscle causing permanent damage or death.
precautions: Begin CPR if person is unconscious and not breathing
Heart attack.treatment: EMERGENCY: Thrombolytics, angioplasty/stent, bypass surgery, cardiac medications, ICU care.
Heart attack.diet: Post-recovery: Mediterranean diet — olive oil, fish, whole grains, vegetables, nuts. Limit red meat, salt.

Varicose veins.description: Enlarged, twisted, bulging veins (usually in legs) due to weakened vein walls and valves.
precautions: Elevate legs above heart level when resting
Varicose veins.treatment: Compression stockings, sclerotherapy, endovenous laser treatment, surgical stripping.
Varicose veins.diet: High fiber, low sodium diet. Foods with rutin (buckwheat, asparagus). Maintain healthy weight.

Hypothyroidism.description: Underactive thyroid gland that produces insufficient thyroid hormone, slowing body functions.
precautions: Take levothyroxine at same time every day on empty stomach
Avoid excess soy and raw cruciferous vegetables
Inform all doctors about your condition
Hypothyroidism.treatment: Levothyroxine (synthetic T4) hormone replacement — lifelong in most cases.
Hypothyroidism.diet: Selenium-rich foods (Brazil nuts, tuna), iodine (seaweed, dairy), zinc. Limit raw kale, broccoli, soy.

Hyperthyroidism.description: Overactive thyroid gland producing excess thyroid hormone, speeding up body functions.
precautions: Manage stress and get adequate rest
Hyperthyroidism.treatment: Antithyroid drugs (methimazole), radioactive iodine therapy, beta-blockers, thyroid surgery.
Hyperthyroidism.diet: Avoid iodine-rich foods (seaweed, iodized salt). Eat cruciferous vegetables, calcium-rich foods for bone health.

Hypoglycemia.description: Dangerously low blood sugar levels causing dizziness, confusion, sweating, and fainting.
precautions: Always carry fast-acting glucose (glucose tablets, juice)
Hypoglycemia.treatment: IMMEDIATE: 15g fast-acting glucose (4oz juice, 3-4 glucose tablets). Follow with complex carbs + protein.
Hypoglycemia.diet: Regular small meals every 3-4 hours. Always combine carbs with protein. Avoid alcohol on empty stomach.

Osteoarthristis.description: Degenerative joint disease where protective cartilage breaks down, causing pain and stiffness.
precautions: Maintain healthy BMI to reduce joint load
Apply heat/cold therapy for pain relief
Osteoarthristis.treatment: NSAIDs, physical therapy, corticosteroid injections, hyaluronic acid injections, joint replacement.
Osteoarthristis.diet: Anti-inflammatory: omega-3 (fish, walnuts), turmeric, ginger, vitamin D and calcium for bone health.

Arthritis.description: Inflammation of one or more joints causing pain, swelling, stiffness, and reduced range of motion.
precautions: Stay active with gentle exercise (yoga, walking)
Apply hot/cold packs for symptom relief
Arthritis.treatment: NSAIDs, DMARDs for rheumatoid arthritis, corticosteroids, biologic agents, physical therapy.
Arthritis.diet: Anti-inflammatory diet: fatty fish, nuts, olive oil, colorful fruits and vegetables. Avoid processed foods.

(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.description: Brief episodes of intense dizziness and spinning sensation triggered by specific head movements.
precautions: Move head and body slowly and deliberately
Avoid sudden position changes (lying down to standing)
(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.treatment: Epley maneuver (canalith repositioning procedure), vestibular rehabilitation exercises.
(vertigo) Paroymsal Positional Vertigo.diet: Low sodium diet reduces fluid retention in inner ear. Stay hydrated. Avoid caffeine and alcohol.

Acne.description: Common skin condition where hair follicles become clogged with oil and dead skin cells.
precautions: Wash face gently twice daily with mild cleanser
Acne.treatment: Topical retinoids, benzoyl peroxide, salicylic acid, antibiotics, isotretinoin for severe cystic acne.
Acne.diet: Low glycemic index diet, reduce dairy intake, eat zinc-rich foods (pumpkin seeds, lentils), omega-3.

Urinary tract infection.description: Bacterial infection in the urinary system (bladder, urethra, kidneys) causing burning and urgency.
precautions: Avoid holding urine for long periods
Urinary tract infection.treatment: Antibiotics (trimethoprim, nitrofurantoin, ciprofloxacin) for 3-7 days; increased fluid intake.
Urinary tract infection.diet: Drink plenty of water and unsweetened cranberry juice. Avoid caffeine, alcohol, and spicy foods.

Psoriasis.description: Chronic autoimmune skin condition causing rapid skin cell buildup resulting in scales and red patches.
precautions: Moisturize skin daily especially after bathing
Identify and avoid personal triggers (stress, infections)
Protect skin from cuts and injuries
Psoriasis.treatment: Topical corticosteroids, vitamin D analogues, biologics (adalimumab), methotrexate, phototherapy.
Psoriasis.diet: Anti-inflammatory diet: omega-3 rich fish, avoid alcohol (major trigger), reduce red meat.

Impetigo.description: Highly contagious superficial bacterial skin infection causing honey-colored crusting sores.
precautions: Keep sores loosely covered with gauze
Don't share towels, clothing, or razors
Keep nails short to avoid scratching and spreading
Impetigo.treatment: Topical mupirocin antibiotic cream for mild cases; oral antibiotics (amoxicillin) for widespread infection.
Impetigo.diet: Vitamin C-rich foods to support skin healing. Adequate protein for immune and tissue repair.
